mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth signals robust economic demand, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in CPI inflation below 2% indicates easing monetary pressure, reducing real interest rate expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in CPIAUCSL < 2.0% periods show negative or neutral performance (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts signals strong supply-side momentum, driving positive equity returns through improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: INDPRO and HOUST growth rates fail to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson r < 0.15) in 60-day rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles, indicated by rising FEDFUNDS and elevated CPIAUCSL, create headwinds for equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in tightening regimes exceed 1.5% despite FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and CPIAUCSL > 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job gains above 100k signal labor market strength, boosting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in PAYEMS > 100k months show negative or neutral performance (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate below 4.0% indicates a tight labor market, increasing wage pressures and corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in UNRATE < 4.0% periods show negative or neutral performance (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) creates favorable conditions for equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: RSAFS and FEDFUNDS fail to jointly predict SPX returns (interaction term p > 0.10) in 60-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: High industrial production and housing starts during late-cycle expansions signal overcapacity risks, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days as earnings growth slows.
failure_condition: INDPRO and HOUST growth rates do not correlate with SPX negative returns (Pearson r > -0.20) in 60-day rolling windows.
